title: QA Python Installation
body:
Week1 - Installation of Python

            Q: I installed Python, and it works in VS Code, but I can't use it in Command
            Prompt/Terminal.



            A: You likely forgot to add Python to the system's PATH during installation. This is
            a common issue, but it’s easy to fix. Simply add Python to the PATH environment
            variable, and you'll be able to run it from Command Prompt/Terminal as well.



            On Mac:
            1> Open Terminal on your Mac.
            2> Input the commands below (the contents after # are annotation, no need to
            execute). This will tell where your python is.

                # zsh shell
                which python3


            3> Add Python to PATH, please note <PATH_TO_PYTHON> is the directory not
            the Python executable itself. For example, the directory in snapshot is /usr/bin not
            /usr/bin/python3




                # replace <PATH_TO_PYTHON> from step2
                echo export PATH="<PATH_TO_PYTHON>:$PATH" >> ~/.zshrc


            4> Reload the configuration file, you’ll make it.




Week1 - Installation of Python                                                                     1
                 source ~/.zshrc




            On Windows:
            If you’re using Windows, it is much easier to add Python to PATH. See the video
            below.


                How to Add Python to PATH – Real Python
                In this tutorial, you'll learn about how to add Python, or any
                other program, to your PATH environment variable. You'll be
                covering the procedure in Windows, macOS, and Linux and find
                    https://realpython.com/add-python-to-path/




Week1 - Installation of Python                                                                2
